name: Story DoR — Data & Reporting Extensions
description: Three additional criteria specific to Data, Reporting & Analytics stories — output artifact, data quality checks, and traceability lineage. Layered on top of the base Story DoR.

criteria.name: Output Artifact Defined
criteria.description: The deliverable (dashboard, report, table, flat file, API) is clearly specified with schema or mockup.
criteria.why: Without a specified artifact, developer and PO may have different expectations at demo.
criteria.fix: Specify the exact deliverable type and include a column list, schema sketch, or mockup link.
criteria.good_example: Dashboard: portfolio delinquency rate filtered by property type and loan vintage. Columns: loan_id, reporting_date, delinquency_rate, property_type. Mockup: [Confluence link].

criteria.name: Data Quality Checks Specified
criteria.description: Row count reconciliation, null checks, and referential integrity requirements are listed.
criteria.why: DQ failures discovered late cause last-minute rework and pipeline rollbacks.
criteria.fix: Add: row count tolerance, which key columns must be non-null, and any FK / referential integrity checks required.
criteria.good_example: Row count reconciles to dw.loan_performance_monthly within 0.01%. No nulls in loan_id or reporting_date. Ref integrity: loan_id must exist in dw.loans.

criteria.name: Data Lineage Documented
criteria.description: Lineage written as Source → Transformation Layer → Output Artifact → Business Consumer.
criteria.why: Enables impact analysis when upstream sources change and supports audit/regulatory requirements.
criteria.fix: Add a single traceability line: [Source System] → [Transformation Layer] → [Output Artifact] → [Business Consumer].
criteria.good_example: EDW.dw.loan_performance → Analytics Mart → Delinquency Dashboard → Risk Committee (weekly pack).
